title: Migrating wireless channels using dual media access controllers
abstract: The claimed subject matter includes techniques for migrating wireless channels. An example method includes initiating, via a first media access controller on an integrated circuit chip, a first connection on a first wireless channel. The example method also includes performing, via a second media access controller on the integrated circuit chip, a scan on additional wireless channels using a second media access controller. The example method also includes detecting, via the second media access controller, an available wireless channel from the additional wireless channels. The method further includes migrating, via the first media access controller, the first connection from the first wireless channel to the available wireless channel. The method further includes combining the second access media controller with the first media access controller to provide increased throughput or signal quality on the available wireless channel.
claims: Claims 1. A system for wireless channel migration, the system being disposed on a single integrated circuit chip, the system comprising: a first media access controller to initiate a first connection on a first wireless channel; and a second media access controller to: perform a scan on additional wireless channels; detect an available wireless channel from the additional wireless channels; send an indicator corresponding to the available wireless channel to the first media controller, the first media controller to send the indication to a client device and migrate the first connection from the first wireless channel to the available wireless channel; and combine with the first media access controller to provide increased throughput or improved wireless signal reception on the available wireless channel. 2. The system of claim 1, the first media access controller to monitor the migrated wireless channel. 3. The system of claim 2, the first media access controller to detect a conflicting use of the migrated wireless channel. 4. The system of claim 3, the first media access controller to migrate the migrated wireless channel to a third wireless channel based on the detected conflicting use. 5. A method for migrating between wireless channels, comprising: initiating, via a first media access controller on an integrated circuit chip, a first connection on a first wireless channel; performing, via a second media access controller on the integrated circuit chip, a scan on additional wireless channels using a second media access controller; detecting, via the second media access controller, an available wireless channel from the additional wireless channels; migrating, via the first media access controller, the first connection from the first wireless channel to the available wireless channel; and combining the second access media controller with the first media access controller to provide increased throughput or signal quality on the available wireless channel. 6. The method of claim 5, further comprising monitoring, via the first media access controller, the migrated wireless channel. 7. The method of claim 6, further comprising detecting, via the first media access controller, a conflicting use of the migrated wireless channel. 8. The method of claim 7, further comprising migrating, via the first media access controller, the migrated wireless channel to a third wireless channel. 9. The method of any combination of claims 7-8, wherein detecting, via the first media access controller, the conflicting use of the migrated wireless channel comprises detecting a radar energy profile. 10. The method of any combination of claims 5-8, further comprising performing a channel availability check, via the second media access controller, on the available wireless channel for a predetermined amount of time before migrating to the available wireless channel via the first media access controller. 1 1. The system of any combination of claims 3-4, the conflicting use of the migrated wireless channel comprising a radar. 12. The system of claim 4, further comprising an energy profile module to detect an energy profile of a priority device and send an indication to the first media access controller, wherein the first and the third wireless channels comprise wireless local area network protocol channels and the second wireless channel comprises a channel of a Dynamic Frequency Selection band. 13. The system of any combination of claims 1-4, wherein the second media access controller is configured to support radio link margin against cancellation effects. 14. The method of any combination of claims 5-10, further comprising operating, via the second media access controller, as a lxl single input, single output (SISO), an NxN multiple input, multiple output (MIMO), or a maximal-ratio combining (MRC) diversity connection with the first media access controller when not performing the scan. 15. The method of any combination of claims 5-8, wherein the first wireless channel comprises a wireless local area network protocol channel and the additional wireless channels comprise Dynamic Frequency Selection (DFS) channels.
